name: dm_control (DeepMind MuJoCo Environments)

performance.notes: MuJoCo C++ backend; headless supported; rendering requires display

agent_readiness.notes: DeepMind's MuJoCo environment suite for RL research. from dm_control import suite. env = suite.load('cartpole', 'balance'). time_step = env.reset(). time_step = env.step(action). time_step.observation. time_step.reward. time_step.discount. Physics: env.physics. Composer API: custom environments from primitives. dm_control.locomotion for locomotion tasks. Pixel observations: env.physics.render(). MJCF model manipulation. Used in research papers (DMControl benchmark). Pairs with Acme, dm-haiku, dm-optax.

gotchas: Requires MuJoCo (mujoco package) — now free but still required
Rendering requires display or virtual framebuffer (Xvfb on Linux)
dm_env TimeStep API differs from Gymnasium — incompatible without wrapper
Composer API for custom environments is complex and poorly documented
best_when: Running DMControl benchmark suite (locomotion, manipulation, cartpole)
DeepMind RL research using Acme + dm-env ecosystem
When pixel-based RL observations from MuJoCo are needed
Custom MJCF-based environment design with Composer API
avoid_when: Gymnasium-compatible environments needed (use mujoco-py or gymnasium-robotics)
Simple RL tasks where standard Gymnasium envs suffice
When not using DeepMind's Acme/JAX ecosystem
summary: dm_control provides DeepMind's MuJoCo environment suite for RL research. suite.load('cartpole', 'balance'). dm_env TimeStep API. Physics access. Pixel rendering. Composer API for custom environments. Locomotion tasks. Apache-2.0 by DeepMind. Standard benchmark for locomotion RL research. TimeStep API differs from Gymnasium — requires wrapper for cross-compatibility. Best for DeepMind Acme/JAX ecosystem.
